title: Tanjay IV
content:

{{Quote|Nemrileo irm-Drocubac (Tanjay IV) died yesterday when his aircar collided with a heavy-equipment delivery vehicle in the S-93 area of the Bindai District.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|51|news/13411_6.html|Tanjay IV Representative Dead in Crash}}</ref>}}
'''Tanjay IV''' was a powerful [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. During the [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]], it was allied with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], Tanjay IV's Republic representative [[Nemrileo irm-Drocubac]] was part of [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] [[Presidente/Legends|Presidente]] [[Shu Mai/Legends|Shu Mai's]] plot to achieve the secession of a number of Republic worlds. However, for his doubts in Mai's leadership, irm-Drocubac was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] in what appeared to be a traffic accident on Coruscant.

==Description==
Tanjay IV,<ref name="CSWE" /> also referred to simply as Tanjay, was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Approaching" /> that occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="CSWE" /> in the [[Tanjay system/Legends|Tanjay system]]. It was a part of the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and lay on the [[Namadii Corridor/Legends|Namadii Corridor]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] that linked it to the [[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Weerden system/Legends|Weerden systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Representative from Tanjay irm-Drocubac leaves behind a wife and two children. Though active in the growing secessionist faction, and suspected of sympathies with the more extreme members of that movement, he was well respected by his colleagues and coworkers, as well as by his supporters on his homeworld.|Coruscant News Network report|<ref name="Approaching" />}}
[[File:TheSpinCloseupAlsakanConflicts-EGTW.jpg|thumb|left|300px|During the conflicts between the Galactic Republic worlds Coruscant and Alsakan, Tanjay IV was allied with the former.]]
Tanjay IV fell within the borders of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as early as [[17,000 BBY]]. During the early [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]], a series of wars that began in [[17,018 BBY]] and saw the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and their respective allies struggle over [[Politics/Legends|political]] and economic control over the Republic, Tanjay IV was affiliated with Coruscant.<ref name="EGTW" />

By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="22 BBY">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Approaching Storm]]'' to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> [[Nemrileo irm-Drocubac]], Tanjay IV's native representative to the Republic, had resided and worked on Coruscant for [[37 BBY/Legends|fifteen years]], being respected by his supporters on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="Approaching" /> That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="22 BBY" /> during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], irm-Drocubac was part of [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] [[Presidente/Legends|Presidente]] [[Shu Mai/Legends|Shu Mai's]] plot to have more Republic worlds secede from it. However, he questioned Mai's loyalty to their cause,<ref name="Approaching" /> and for those doubts, he was [[Death/Legends|killed]]<ref name="CSWE" /> in what was staged to look like a traffic accident on Coruscant.<ref name="Approaching" /> The accident was subsequently reported on by an issue of the [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District Edition]] of [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] 13:4:11<ref name="HNN" /> as well as by a newsblink of the [[Coruscant News Network]], which erroneously referred to Tanjay IV as "Tanjay VI." Both reports mentioned irm-Drocubac's homeworld,<ref name="Approaching" /><ref name="HNN" /> with the Coruscant News Network story also noting that, per Tanjay tradition, the representative's ashes would be scattered over [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]] the following [[Standard day/Legends|day]].<ref name="Approaching" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|In accordance with Tanjay tradition, ''[Nemrileo irm-Drocubac's]'' ashes will be scattered tomorrow above the capital city where he lived and worked for the past fifteen years of his life.|Coruscant News Network report|<ref name="Approaching" />}}
By the time of the Separatist Crisis, Tanjay IV was a powerful world. Its inhabitants observed a tradition of scattering a deceased person's ashes over a [[City/Legends|city]].<ref name="Approaching" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:TheApproachingStorm-Hardcover.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Tanjay IV was introduced in ''The Approaching Storm''.]]
Tanjay IV was first mentioned in [[Alan Dean Foster]]'s<ref name="Approaching" /> [[January 29]], [[2002]]<ref name="Amazon">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/novel/f20020129/index.html|text=''Approaching Storm'' Interview and Excerpt}}</ref> novel ''[[The Approaching Storm]]''. In the novel, the full name of the world is given as "Tanjay VI;"<ref name="Approaching" /> however, it is instead referred to as Tanjay IV by both [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 55|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #55]], a representation of an [[in-universe]] issue of the HoloNet News<ref name="HNN" /> written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> and published on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]] on [[April 11]], 2002,<ref name="HNN51 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6401165|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=MattPeriolat|date=2002-04-11|time=6:19 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200305125429/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-13}}</ref> and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. This article assumes the latter designation to be correct and the former name to be an in-universe error.<ref name="CSWE" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Tanjay [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Tanjay IV, in grid square K-9.<ref name="TEA" />
